doc_title: SRM-COMSECY-20-0012: Enclosure 1 - Memorandum of Understanding Between the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission on Uranium in situ Recovery Activities

steps.text: Keep the other Party generally informed of relevant plans and schedules, and respond to requests for information.
Make staff members readily available for collaboration, information sharing, and resolution of concerns.
Work in good faith to resolve concerns in a timely manner.
Treat drafts of proposed or final rules and related documents as pre-decisional and deliberative information.

NRC requests the concurrence of the EPA in writing from the Director, Office of Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, to the EPA through the Director, Office of Radiation and Indoor Air, including a copy of the draft final rule.
Make all reasonable efforts to resolve the disagreement as collegially and quickly as possible.
Raise resolution of the disagreement to the Office of the Executive Director for Operations (NRC) and the Assistant Administrator for Air and Radiation (EPA).
Designate a representative and alternate representative.
Provide written notice to the other Party at least 30 days in advance of the desired termination date.

preconditions.text: If the EPA chooses to promulgate standards of general application under its authority in AEA § 275
Before the promulgation of any standards of general application
If the NRC chooses to promulgate regulations for regulating ISR activities
If an NRC rulemaking would promulgate general requirements for the management of byproduct material
If a disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence
If necessary to resolve a disagreement

constraints.text: Neither Party will disclose or transmit draft regulations or other related documents to any third party unless expressly agreed to or required by law.
All commitments made by the EPA and the NRC in this MOU are subject to the availability of appropriated funds and budget priorities.
